summary: Kinnship Build #55 (Me tab full rewrite + PATCH /auth/me + preferences.location_sharing_enabled + PIN session + biometrics scaffolding + /settings removal) — end-to-end test. BACKEND: 18/18 pytest cases pass in /app/backend/tests/test_build55_me_profile_prefs.py — all PATCH /auth/me validation branches (name <2 → 400, name >80 → 400, invalid IANA tz → 400, whitespace-trim, full-name-only, timezone-only, both, empty-no-op, auth required), all /me/preferences branches (GET default location_sharing_enabled=True, PUT partial location_sharing_enabled, PUT partial quiet_hours preserves location_sharing_enabled, PUT both together, auth required), and regression (GET /auth/me, PUT /auth/timezone happy+400, POST /auth/request-otp + verify-otp OTP round-trip creates a fresh user). FRONTEND: /(tabs)/me renders every expected section (Account → Plan → Notifications → Security → Privacy → Legal → Advanced → Sign out → Danger Zone → Footer) with correct testIDs. Name edit modal saves via PATCH /auth/me and updates the row live. Timezone modal: 'Not/AZone' surfaces the backend 400 detail inline; 'America/Los_Angeles' saves. Free-tier plan card shows me-view-plans + me-manage-plan-free and hides the paid-tier me-manage-plan (correct). Security section shows me-setup-pin only (no PIN saved), hiding me-change-pin / me-remove-pin / me-biometrics (correct — biometrics also invisible on web, per spec). Location-sharing switch toggles ON→OFF and the backend GET /api/me/preferences returns location_sharing_enabled=false immediately after — full round-trip confirmed. Delete-account modal opens on tap; confirm button aria-disabled=true until user types DELETE, then becomes enabled. Legacy /settings route: no source references remain (grep of app/ src/ clean), app/settings.tsx removed, visiting /settings renders the Expo unmatched-route 404 gracefully (no runtime error). Login/PIN gate reachable via localStorage['kc_token']+'@kinnship/pin_setup_dismissed_v1' — dashboard reachable, Me tab reachable through bottom bar. pin-session key '@kinnship/pin_session_v1_<uid>' absent when no PIN unlock occurred in-session (correct — the key is only written by verifyPin, and we bypassed PIN via the dismissed-setup flag). Zero deprecated Expo packages triggered on Me screen (only expo-notifications 'Listening to push token changes not fully supported on web' warning — pre-existing / unrelated / harmless per Expo docs).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:1556 PATCH /auth/me correctly trims full_name before length-check, so '  A  ' → 'A' → 400 (verified by test_reject_name_too_short). Boundary math (len<2 or len>80) matches the spec verbatim.

server.py:1600-1602 GET /me/preferences defensively coerces missing/None location_sharing_enabled to True — preserves pre-Build-#55 behaviour for existing users. Confirmed with a fresh-user OTP signup.

server.py:1609-1638 PUT /me/preferences is genuinely partial — only fields present in the request body land in set_doc. test_put_partial_quiet_hours_only_preserves_location_sharing explicitly verifies the OTHER field is preserved after a partial write (the historical regression class for partial-PUT endpoints).

me.tsx:214 sets locSharing to the AND of server value and local flag, and me.tsx:215 forwards the SERVER value to setLocationSharingEnabled() — the server is the source of truth on tab focus, which is the right precedence for a two-device account.
me.tsx:307 the frontend still writes the server FIRST, then the local flag, then stops/starts the OS background task — matches the review-request order and the comments in-file.

_layout.tsx:164 PIN gate now honours isSessionValid(user.id) — a persisted 24h PIN session survives Android low-memory JS reclaim (the specific class of bug this build targets). Cannot be exercised on web (no PIN unlock ran), but the code path compiled and mounted without error.

The `me-manage-plan` testID is only rendered when billing.plan === 'family_plan'; the free-tier alternate button is `me-manage-plan-free`. The review request said 'me-view-plans (free) or me-manage-plan (paid tier)' — spec matches implementation, but note both a paid-tier `me-manage-plan` AND a free-tier `me-manage-plan-free` exist. Not a defect, just documenting.

success_rate.frontend: 100% for the flows exercised on web (name edit, tz edit valid+invalid, location-sharing toggle round-trip to backend, delete-modal DELETE-gate, legacy /settings 404, tab order & testID inventory)

seed_data_creation: test_otp_signup_roundtrip_creates_fresh_user creates ONE disposable user with email build55-regression-<epoch>@example.com via the real OTP flow (grep of /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log for the 6-digit code). No password. No family group is created for that user (verify-otp is signup-only in this test). The alice_baseline fixture snapshots Alice's name/timezone/quiet_hours/location_sharing_enabled before the suite and restores them in teardown, so successive runs are idempotent.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run `pytest backend/tests/test_build55_me_profile_prefs.py -v` for Build 55 regression. Auth to Alice's account on web via: (1) localStorage['kc_token']=<Alice JWT from /app/memory/test_credentials.md line 11>, (2) localStorage['disclaimer_accepted']='1', (3) localStorage['onboarding_done_v1']='1', (4) localStorage['@kinnship/pin_setup_dismissed_v1']=JSON.stringify({[uid]:Date.now()}) — this is the ONLY dismissed-flag key that _layout.tsx currently reads via wasPinSetupDismissed (from src/pinSetupPrompt.ts line 13: KEY='@kinnship/pin_setup_dismissed_v1'). The older `pin_setup_dismissed_<uid>` key mentioned in test_credentials.md line 28 does NOT work on Build #55. Also NOTE: the RN Web `<Switch>` component does NOT expose `aria-checked` — assert toggle behaviour via server round-trip (GET /api/me/preferences) instead of DOM state. To reach the Me tab from a fresh page, prime the four localStorage keys above then navigate to /dashboard and click the bottom-bar 'Me' text (Expo Router (tabs) group is not directly URL-navigable on web — visiting /(tabs)/me redirects to /dashboard).
rca_of_issue: No open bugs. Build #55 acceptance criteria fully met across both backend and frontend.
